rulings.Case Title: SF URBAN FOREST COALITION, A CALIFORNIA VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A CHARTER CITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing Re: Petition For Writ Of Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2018, Line 1, Hearing Re: Petition For Writ Of Mandamus. Hearing required unless the parties have resolved all issues relating to the third cause of action for violation of the California Public Records Act, including the issues of whether petitioner SF Urban Forest Coalition has standing and whether petitioner has received all documents it is entitled to receive per the CPRA. If the parties have resolved all issues relating to the third cause of action, the hearing on the petition for writ of mandamus of petitioner as to the first and second causes of action is continued on the court's own motion to May 9, 2018 for ruling on the merits of those claims. While the court does not require any further briefing on the issue of whether respondent SFTCA is subject to the Sunshine Ordinance, the parties are permitted to provide short additional memorandum of no more than ten pages on that issue no later than May 2, 2018. The first and second causes of action are not moot due to the SFCTA's position that it is not subject to the Sunshine Ordinance, which is sufficient to establish an actual controversy per CCP 1060. (Environmental Defense Project of Sierra County v. County of Sierra (2008) 158 Cal. App. 4th 877; California Alliance for Utilities Safety and Education v. City of San Diego (1997) 56 Cal. App. 4th 1024). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SF URBAN FOREST COALITION, A CALIFORNIA VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A CHARTER CITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2018, Line 1, RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. Hearing required unless the parties have resolved all issues relating to the third cause of action for violation of the California Public Records Act, including the issues of whether petitioner SF Urban Forest Coalition has standing and whether petitioner has received all documents it is entitled to receive per the CPRA. If the parties have resolved all issues relating to the third cause of action, the demurrer of respondents SFCTA, Tily Chang and Cynthia Fong is overruled as to the first and second causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action. The first and second causes of action are not moot due to the SFCTA's position that it is not subject to the Sunshine Ordinance, which is sufficient to establish an actual controversy per CCP 1060. (Environmental Defense Project of Sierra County v. County of Sierra (2008) 158 Cal. App. 4th 877; California Alliance for Utilities Safety and Education v. City of San Diego (1997) 56 Cal. App. 4th 1024). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the petitioner is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SERGE ELMAN ET AL VS. BAY CLUB PENINSULA, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-17-559235 WITH CASE CGC-14-542559
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFFs SERGE ELMAN, ELENA PHILLIPS' MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-17-559235 WITH CASE CGC-14-542559. Plaintiffs Serge Elman and Elena Phillips' motion to consolidate this case and case CGC-17-559235 is granted and the trial date for the two consolidated cases is now November 5, 2018 at 9:30am in department 206. The two cases involve numerous common questions of law and fact and consolidation furthers judicial economy and avoidance of unnecessary costs. All deadlines for both consolidated cases, including discovery cutoff dates, are trigged off the November 5 trial date. Consolidation is not inconsistent with ruling on defendants' demurrer in case CGC-17-559235 nor is it an impermissible collateral attack on the prior ruling denying Ms. Phillips' request to add claims to this case. Rather, the delay in resolution of this case, for whatever reason, now affords the opportunity to have all claims tried together and the consolidation statute provides the vehicle to do so. Any prejudice to defendants by consolidation has been eliminated by continuing the trial date to November 5, 2018. The two cases are consolidated for all purposes. This case is the lead case and all further filings must be in this case only. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. RALPH TASHJIAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFF FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY'S Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment. Plaintiff Fidelity National Title Insurance Company's application for right to attach order and writ of attachemnt against defendants Ralph and Valerie Tashjian is denied. Construing the statutory language strictly as required by case law (Pacific Decision Sciences Corp. v. Superior Court (2004) 121 Cal. App. 4th 1100, 1106), Fidelity's claim for breach of implied warranty of title is "based on the sale ¿ of property ¿ where the property sold ¿was used by the defendant primarily for personal, family, or household purposes"(CCP 483.010(c)) and thus is not a claim for which an attachment may be issued. (Id.). But for the Tashjians' sale of their home to Fidelity's insured, Fidelity would have no claim. The Tashjians' obligations per the implied warranty of title doctrine arose from the sale of their home and their decision to secure a loan by a deed of trust against their home. The fact that the loan was a "business loan" does not convert the sale transaction which triggered the warranty obligation from a sale of a residence to a business transaction. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: POLK STREET GROUP LLC VS. BITTERS BOCK & RYE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief For Untimely Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2018, Line 7, Plaintiff Polk Street Group, Llc.'s Motion For Relief For Untimely Motion To Compel. Pro Tem Judge Dianne Peebles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The PARTIES are ORDERED to meet and confer on a PROTECTIVE ORDER and on what document production the Parties can agree on and to APPEAR at the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dpeebleslaw@comcast.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KELLY KIMBALL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Plaintiff'S Third Cause Of Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANTS XELAN PROP 1, LLC, ANNA KIHAGI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Plaintiff'S Third Cause Of Action. Defendants Anna Kihagi and Xelan Prop 1, LLC's motion for summary adjudication of the third cause of action for breach of warranty of quiet enjoyment is denied. Liberally construing the evidence of plaintiff Kelly Kimball, his declaration creates a triable issue whether the asserted conduct of Ms. Kihagi staring at Mr. Kimball was a substantial breach of the warranty of quiet enjoyment. Defendants' objections to the portions of Mr. Kimball's declaration regarding the staring are overruled since defendants have not shown that there is anything irreconcilable between the declaration and Mr. Kimball's prior testimony regarding the staring. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Kimball is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and complies with CCP 437c(g) and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: EVAN KROW VS. SAUL GEVERTZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANT SAUL GEVERTZ'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Saul Gevertz's demurrer to all five causes of action in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Evan Krow is sustained with twenty days leave to amend to all five causes of action. All five causes of action fail based on either direct liability or alter ego liability for the conduct of Fox Ortega Enterprises due to insufficient allegations of required elements. Direct liability for the breach of contract and common counts claims fails due to the absence of any allegation that Mr. Gevertz was a contracting party. Direct liability on the misrepresentation claims fails due to the absence of any allegation that Mr. Gevertz personally made any misrepresentation that was relied on by Mr. Krow. Direct liability on the negligence claim fails due to the absence of any allegations of facts showing that Mr. Gevertz owed a duty sounding in negligence law to Mr. Krow. Alter ego liability fails due to the absence of sufficient allegations showing that there was a unity of interest between Mr. Gevertz and Fox Ortega Enterprises such that in reality they were one and the same. If he can do so in good faith, Mr. Krow is given one last opportunity to correct the deficiencies in his claims and, if he chooses to do so, Mr. Krow must allege facts showing that the claims are not time-barred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Gevertz is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHEW KAM KUNG VS. JINCONG ZHEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT JINCONG ZHEN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Jincong Zhen's demurrer to plaintiff Chew Kung's complaint is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Mr. Kung is granted leave to amend to allege facts regarding the creation and nature (written, oral, or implied), and terms of the contract he entered into with Mr. Zhen. Mr. Kung should also allege how he performed the contract, how Mr. Zhen breached it, and how Mr. Kung was damaged. If the contract was written, Mr. Kung should attach a copy if practical. Mr. Kung also has leave to plead any other appropriate cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DORIAN LISBONA VS. EFRAIM SHOUA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CPF-18-516073 WITH CASE CGC-17-559401
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2018, Line 10, PLAINTIFF DORIAN LISBONA'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CPF-18-516073 WITH CASE CGC-17-559401. Plaintiff Dorian Lisbona's motion to consolidate this case with case CPF-18-516073 is granted on the condition that Mr. Lisbona not file a motion to continue the trial. The two cases are consolidated for all purposes and the trial date for both consolidated cases is May 14, 2018. This case is the lead case and all further filings must be in this case only. Defendant Efraim Shoua stated that he does not oppose consolidation as long as the trial date for both cases is May 14, 2018. If Mr. Lisbona does not accept the condition that he not file a motion to continue the trial, this motion is denied because of the very different stages that the two cases are in. By failing to contest the tentative ruling and/or submitting a proposed order as required by this tentative ruling, Mr. Lisbona assents to the condition that he not file a motion to continue the trial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Lisbona is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SHARON SALDAVIA VS. MACY'S CORPORATE SERVICES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Further Responses From Macys West Stores, Inc. Re Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2018, Line 8, Plaintiff Sharon Saldavia's Motion To Compel Further Responses From Macys West Stores, Inc. Re Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Dianne Peebles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The PARTIES are ORDERED to APPEAR to address Plaintiff's Reply to Defendant's Opposition to Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Requests For Production, Set One and the issue of whether there were misrepresentations to the court (Business and Professions Code section 6068 (d)). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dpeebleslaw@comcast.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA OLLA VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2018, Line 14, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant SFUSD's demurrer to the sole cause of action in plaintiff Maria Olla's complaint is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Ms. Olla is granted leave to plead separate causes of action for disability discrimination, failure to accommodate disability, and failure to engage in the interactive process with more particularity. (Government Code 12940(a), (m), and (n).) Ms. Olla should allege facts regarding the nature of her mental disability, how it limits a major life activity, and SFUSD's awareness of her disability. Ms. Olla also has leave to clarify that the adverse employment actions were SFUSD's failure to provide her with a position in July 2016 and subsequently medically separating her. Ms. Olla should remove her allegations relating to October 2011 as she acknowledges that the conduct is not a basis for liability. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for SFUSD is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOEL DROTTS VS. SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause For Preliminary Injunction. (Order To Be Served By 4-17-2018; Opposition By 4-20-2018; Reply By 4-23-2018) (See Scanned Order)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2018, Line 16, Plaintiff's Order To Show Cause For Preliminary Injunction. Plaintiff Joel Drotts' petition for preliminary injunction is denied. Mr. Drotts does not demonstrate a likelihood of prevailing on the merits. Mr. Drotts does not show a due process violation. The authorities he cites approve of post-tow hearings. (Goichman v. Rheuban Motors, Inc. (9th Cir. 1982) 682 F.2d 1320, 1325; Drotts' Ex Parte Petition, 52:16-21.) Mr. Drotts is unlikely to prevail on his conversion claim. Mr. Drotts admits that the nonprofit corporation owns the Lincoln. (Kawala Dec., Ex. 3.) The certificate of title that is attached to the Kawala declaration also indicates that someone other than Mr. Drotts owns the Volvo. Mr. Drotts needs an ownership interest to maintain the conversion claim. Lastly, the Kawala declaration indicates that many of the citations have not been paid and thus the tows were warranted and the balance of equities and harms does not weigh in favor of Mr. Drotts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
